articles.author: Matthew  Ablon
articles.title: Norovirus outbreak reported on Princess cruise ship that departed Fort Lauderdale - CBS News
articles.description: This is the second time within two months that a norovirus outbreak happened on a Princess ship based out of the Fort Lauderdale.
articles.content:
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports it is monitoring a norovirus outbreak on board a Princess Cruises ship that departed Fort Lauderdale more than a week ago.

articles.author: Kierra  Frazier
articles.title: Arizona man who survived hantavirus 24 years ago, but lost mom and sister, says recent outbreak is "hard" to process - CBS News
articles.description: In 2002, Zermeño found out he contracted hantavirus after cleaning the family house following the death of his mother and sister. He had been exposed to rodent droppings and became infected.
articles.content: Twenty-four years ago, Arizona photojournalist Gilbert Zermeño, who contracted hantavirus after losing both his mother and sister to the illness, says news of the recent outbreak has been hard to pro… [+2115 chars]

articles.author: Maria Cohut, Ph.D.
articles.title: Is it true that eating more eggs could help lower Alzheimer's risk? - Medical News Today
articles.description: People who eat eggs at least five times a week appear to have a lower risk of Alzheimer's disease but experts caution that there are more factors to take into account.
articles.content: <ul><li>Some existing research has suggested that egg consumption could benefit brain health as we age, with one recent study indicating that eating one egg per week was linked to lower Alzheimers ri… [+10518 chars]

articles.title: I’m an ICU Doctor Who Treated a Critically Ill Hantavirus Patient. Here’s What Everyone Should Know. - Women's Health
articles.description: An ICU physician who treated a hantavirus patient explains how the virus spreads, why it can become deadly so quickly, and when to seek emergency medical care.
